req.title: Manager creates org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a org-announcement through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-049 covers a UI form flow for org-announcement creation with mandatory-field validation and audit-record generation. Five tests are derived: (1) EP-positive – a manager submits all valid mandatory fields and receives 200/201 plus an audit record; (2) NEG-negative – a manager omits a mandatory field and expects a 400 validation error visible in the UI; (3) BVA-boundary – the title field is tested at its maximum-length boundary (255 chars) to confirm acceptance; (4) STC-positive – state-transition test verifying that an unauthenticated user is redirected to login (the system correctly transitions from unauthenticated state to the login screen, which is a positive assertion about the authentication gate behaviour, not a negative test of the feature itself); (5) DT-negative – a viewer-role user is authenticated but forbidden, expecting 403. The violation fix is in test[3]: the unauthenticated-redirect test was marked kind=negative with heuristic=STC. STC (State Transition Coverage) tests must be kind=positive because they assert the system correctly follows a defined state transition path. The kind is corrected to 'positive' while all other fields are preserved.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_org_announcement_valid_payload
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements/new
Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the form
Assert response/redirect indicates success (200 or 201)
Assert entity id is present in response or URL
Assert audit record exists for the created announcement
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 returned or page redirects to detail view
Announcement entity id visible in page URL or API response
Audit record written and retrievable via audit API
trace: R-WEB-049: audit record written on success
R-WEB-049: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_creates_org_announcement_valid_payload(
    manager_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for manager role
    api_client,          # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session for manager
    announcement_data,   # <fixture>: dict with valid mandatory field values
    audit_api,           # <fixture>: helper to query audit records
    base_url,            # <fixture>: e.g. "https://hr-app.local"
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements/new")

    # <fill_announcement_form>(page, announcement_data)  # <helper_fn>

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("POST", "GET")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    # entity_id = <extract_entity_id>(page, response)  # <helper_fn>
    # assert entity_id, "Entity id must be present after creation"

    # audit_records = audit_api.get_records(entity_type="org_announcement", entity_id=entity_id)
    # assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on success"
    # assert audit_records[0]["action"] == "CREATE"

tests.name: test_manager_gets_400_when_mandatory_field_missing
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements/new
Leave the mandatory 'title' field empty
Fill all other mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the form
Assert validation error is displayed in the UI
Assert no entity is created (no entity id in URL or response)
expected: Form displays a validation error message for the missing field
HTTP 400 or inline form error rendered (no redirect to detail view)
No org-announcement entity created in the system
trace: R-WEB-049: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_gets_400_when_mandatory_field_missing(
    manager_page,
    announcement_data,
    audit_api,
    base_url,
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements/new")

    incomplete = {k: v for k, v in announcement_data.items() if k != "title"}
    # <fill_announcement_form>(page, incomplete)  # <helper_fn>

    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r) if "/org-announcements" in r.url else None)
    page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()
    page.wait_for_timeout(1000)

    post_responses = [r for r in responses if r.request.method == "POST"]
    if post_responses:
        assert post_responses[-1].status == 400, "Expected HTTP 400 for missing mandatory field"
    else:
        error_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-title'], .field-error, [role='alert']")
        expect(error_locator).to_be_visible()

    assert "/org-announcements/" not in page.url or "new" in page.url, \
        "Should not redirect to detail view on validation failure"

tests.name: test_manager_creates_announcement_with_max_length_title
preconditions: Max allowed title length is 255 characters (per data model)
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements/new
Set 'title' field to exactly 255 characters
Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the form
Assert creation succeeds with 200 or 201
Assert entity id returned
expected: Form accepts 255-character title without truncation or error
Announcement created with full 255-character title preserved
trace: R-WEB-049: mandatory fields must be validated (BVA upper boundary for title)
R-WEB-049: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255

def test_manager_creates_announcement_with_max_length_title(
    manager_page,
    announcement_data,
    api_client,
    base_url,
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements/new")

    boundary_data = dict(announcement_data)
    boundary_data["title"] = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    # <fill_announcement_form>(page, boundary_data)  # <helper_fn>

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method in ("POST", "GET")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), \
        f"Expected 200/201 for max-length title, got {response.status}"

    # entity_id = <extract_entity_id>(page, response)  # <helper_fn>
    # assert entity_id, "Entity id must be present for boundary-valid submission"

    # detail = api_client.get(f"{base_url}/api/org-announcements/{entity_id}").json()
    # assert len(detail["title"]) == MAX_TITLE_LEN, "Title must not be truncated"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_org_announcement
actions: Attempt to navigate directly to /org-announcements/new without authentication
Observe the system state transition: expect redirect to login page or HTTP 401 response
Verify the system has correctly transitioned to the unauthenticated-redirect state
expected: System correctly transitions unauthenticated request to the login/401 state
User is redirected to login page OR receives HTTP 401
trace: R-WEB-049: precondition – authenticated (STC verifies the authentication gate state transition fires correctly)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_org_announcement(
    browser,   # <fixture>: raw Playwright browser (no auth)
    base_url,
):
    context = browser.new_context()
    page = context.new_page()
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements/new")
    page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")

    is_redirected_to_login = "login" in page.url or "signin" in page.url or "auth" in page.url
    has_401 = False
    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r))
    if not is_redirected_to_login:
        status_responses = [r for r in responses if "/org-announcements" in r.url]
        has_401 = any(r.status == 401 for r in status_responses)

    assert is_redirected_to_login or has_401, \
        "Unauthenticated access must result in redirect to login or HTTP 401"

    if not is_redirected_to_login:
        form_locator = page.locator("form[action*='org-announcements']")
        expect(form_locator).not_to_be_visible()

    context.close()

tests.name: test_viewer_role_forbidden_from_creating_org_announcement
preconditions: User is authenticated with role=viewer (insufficient privileges)
Browser session fixture is available for viewer role
actions: Navigate to /org-announcements/new as viewer
Attempt to submit the form or observe access denial
Assert HTTP 403 or access-denied UI message is displayed
Assert no org-announcement entity is created
expected: HTTP 403 returned or access-denied page rendered
No org-announcement form is accessible or submission is rejected
No audit record for a CREATE action
trace: R-WEB-049: precondition – role:manager (DT covers the decision where role != manager)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_viewer_role_forbidden_from_creating_org_announcement(
    viewer_page,   # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for viewer role
    base_url,
    audit_api,
):
    page = viewer_page

    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r) if "/org-announcements" in r.url else None)

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/org-announcements/new")
    page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")

    forbidden_responses = [r for r in responses if r.status == 403]
    access_denied_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='access-denied'], .forbidden, [role='alert']")

    assert forbidden_responses or access_denied_locator.count() > 0, \
        "Viewer role must receive 403 or access-denied UI when accessing org-announcement creation"

    assert "/org-announcements/" not in page.url or "new" in page.url or "forbidden" in page.url, \
        "Viewer must not reach the announcement detail view"
